root_text.an6.76:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.76:1.3: Mānaṁ, omānaṁ, atimānaṁ, adhimānaṁ, thambhaṁ, atinipātaṁ.
root_text.an6.76:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.76:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.76:2.3: Mānaṁ, omānaṁ, atimānaṁ, adhimānaṁ, thambhaṁ, atinipātaṁ.
root_text.an6.76:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.76:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up six things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.76:1.3: Conceit, inferiority complex, superiority complex, overestimation, obstinacy, and groveling.
translation_text.an6.76:1.4: Without giving up these six qualities you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.76:2.1: After giving up six things you can realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.76:2.3: Conceit, inferiority complex, superiority complex, overestimation, obstinacy, and groveling.
translation_text.an6.76:2.4: After giving up these six things you can realize perfection.”
